reasoning: The consensus tracking from NRG and BoxOfficePro puts 'A Minecraft Movie' roughly around a $55M - $75M domestic opening weekend (pegged specifically at $58M by NRG). Movies opening in this range outside of the holiday corridor almost never gross $1 billion worldwide, usually finishing in the $350M–$500M tier globally (similar to the Sonic the Hedgehog movies and Detective Pikachu). The few films to cross $1B off such an opening, like Zootopia or Aquaman, had distinct advantages like holiday legs or near-perfect reception. Conversely, 'A Minecraft Movie' has faced starkly negative reactions to its trailers, creating significant headwinds for strong word-of-mouth or 'legs'. With presales currently pacing behind Sonic 3 and FNAF — both of which maxed out well below $400M worldwide — there is no empirical pathway for the film to reach $1 billion globally without an unprecedented, all-time historic tracking error coupled with staggering international reception. The previous Manifold market value of 0.20 on March 6 likely reflects early optimism based purely on the huge IP footprint before this concrete tracking data shattered that thesis on March 13. My final estimate is 3%.
